unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities
topic: Systems of Linear Inequalities by Graphing

questions.question: Which system of linear inequalities has the solution region shown?
In a space lab, the temperature $T$ and humidity $H$ must satisfy $T \geq 20$ and $H \leq 60$. What is the feasible region?

options.A: upper left corner of the coordinate plane
options.B: lower right corner of the coordinate plane
options.C: upper right corner of the coordinate plane
options.D: lower left corner of the coordinate plane
options.E: all four corners of the coordinate plane

questions.question: What is the solution to the system $2x + 3y \geq 12$ and $x - 2y \leq -3$?

options.E: all points above the line $2x + 3y = 12$ and below the line $x - 2y = -3$

questions.question: A spacecraft requires $x \geq 5$ and $y \leq 10$ to navigate safely. Which point satisfies these conditions?

The gravitational force $F$ between two objects is given by $F \leq \frac{Gm_1m_2}{r^2}$. If $G = 6.674 \times 10^{-11}$, $m_1 = 5.972 \times 10^{24}$, and $m_2 = 7.348 \times 10^{22}$, what is the maximum gravitational force?
What is the equation of the boundary line for the inequality $3x - 2y \geq 5$?
A physicist is modeling the trajectory of a projectile with the inequalities $y \geq x - 2$ and $y \leq -x + 4$. What is the solution region?

options.A: the region above the line $y = x - 2$
options.C: the region between the lines $y = x - 2$ and $y = -x + 4$
options.D: the region outside the lines $y = x - 2$ and $y = -x + 4$
options.E: the region below the line $y = x - 2$ and above the line $y = -x + 4$

questions.question: Which inequality represents the region below the line $y = 2x - 3$?
Graph the system of linear inequalities $x \geq 2$ and $y \leq -x + 3$. Identify the feasible region and provide the coordinates of the vertices.
Solve the system $x + 2y \geq 4$ and $3x - 2y \leq 5$. Provide the solution region and the equations of the boundary lines.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of feasible regions.
Tip 2: Encourage students to visualize the solution regions.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their work by graphing the inequalities.
